en: The thing about Americans that I've thought about the language is that they speak... They say they speak English,
ko: 미국인들에 대해 제가 언어적으로 생각해본 건, 그들이 말하길... 영어를 한다고 하잖아요,

en: but they've had to change it to make them understand it more.
ko: 근데 자기들이 더 잘 이해하려고 바꿔버렸어요.

en: Go with me on this, because I've thought it through. They've changed some of the words.
ko: 한번 따라와 보세요, 저도 깊이 생각해봤거든요. 단어 몇 개를 바꿨어요.

en: So, they've taken the English language, but they've looked at some of it and they've thought,
ko: 그러니까, 영어를 가져다가 일부를 보고 이렇게 생각한 거예요,

en: no, no, I think we need a little bit more explanation here, OK?
ko: 아니, 아니, 여기 설명이 좀 더 필요한 것 같아, 알겠지?

en: That's my American accent. So, things like pavement.
ko: 이게 제 미국식 억양이에요. 그러니까, 'pavement' 같은 거요.

en: So, they can't work with pavement, so they've changed it to sidewalk.
ko: pavement으로는 안 되니까, sidewalk으로 바꿨어요.

en: They needed more information. They needed to know where they were going to be walking
ko: 정보가 더 필요했던 거예요. 어디서 걸어야 하는지 알아야 했으니까

en: so that they wouldn't get run over. So, that's... I didn't think of that. Pavement, sidewalk.
ko: 차에 안 치이려고요. 그래서... 그건 생각 못 했네. Pavement, sidewalk.

en: There must have been a period of time where they ran with the word pavement, but they kept getting hit by cars.
ko: pavement이라는 단어를 쓰던 시기가 있었을 텐데, 계속 차에 치였을 거예요.

en: So, they changed it to sidewalk. Now, bear with me here, Jon, because I've thought this through.
ko: 그래서 sidewalk으로 바꿨죠. 자, 좀만 참아주세요 존, 이거 깊이 생각해봤거든요.

en: Bin. The word for bin. In American, waste paper basket.
ko: Bin. bin이라는 단어. 미국에선 waste paper basket이에요.

en: They needed to know not only what to put in it,
ko: 뭘 넣어야 하는지뿐만 아니라,

en: they needed to know that not only it was paper, but waste paper, and then it goes into a basket.
ko: 종이만이 아니라 버릴 종이라는 것도 알아야 했고, 바구니에 넣는다는 것도요.

en: It's not just any paper. They kept throwing away fresh paper for a period
ko: 아무 종이나 아니에요. 한동안 새 종이를 계속 버렸대요

en: before they introduced the word waste paper basket. You see, it's like they need instructions.
ko: waste paper basket이라는 말을 도입하기 전에요. 보세요, 설명서가 필요한 거예요.

en: Glasses for your eyes. They called them eyeglasses.
ko: 눈에 쓰는 glasses. 미국에선 eyeglasses라고 불렀어요.

en: They needed to know where to put them on their face.
ko: 얼굴 어디에 써야 하는지 알아야 했거든요.

en: Because they used to have glasses, and they would put them on their thighs, and they would put them on their feet,
ko: 왜냐면 glasses가 있으면 허벅지에도 올리고, 발에도 올리고,

en: and they would say, I can't see any better with those glasses.
ko: 이러면서 말하겠죠, 이 안경 써도 더 안 보이는데.

en: And then somebody went, no, they're eyeglasses. Well, why didn't you call them eyeglasses?
ko: 그러면 누군가가, 아니 그건 eyeglasses야. 그럼 왜 처음부터 eyeglasses라고 안 했어?

en: There's a game called Squash. Have you heard of it?
ko: 스쿼시라는 운동 있잖아요. 들어보셨죠?

en: Yes. Not in America. Racketball. They needed...
ko: 네. 미국에선 아니에요. 라켓볼이에요. 그들은...

en: It's a true story. They needed to know what they were going to be playing with,
ko: 실화예요. 뭘로 치는지 알아야 했어요,

en: a racquetball, and even then they get confused, because there's no court.
ko: 라켓볼, 근데 그래도 헷갈려해요, 코트가 없으니까.

en: They don't know where to go. They just wander through the street going,
ko: 어디로 가야 할지 모르니까. 그냥 거리를 돌아다니면서,

en: I want to play racquetball, I want to play racquetball. So the company decided to change that.
ko: 라켓볼 하고 싶어, 라켓볼 하고 싶어. 그래서 회사에서 바꾸기로 한 거예요.

en: But my favourite one, without a shadow of a doubt, is horse riding.
ko: 근데 제가 제일 좋아하는 건, 의심의 여지 없이, 승마예요.

en: Do you know what horse riding is in America? Is it not called horse riding? No. Horseback riding.
ko: 미국에서 승마를 뭐라고 하는지 아세요? horse riding 아니에요? 아니요. Horseback riding이에요.

en: LAUGHTER They had to specify the area of the horse.
ko: 말의 어느 부위인지 특정해야 했어요.

en: Well, yes. There was terrible problems,
ko: 네, 맞아요. 끔찍한 문제가 있었거든요,

en: because there was a period of time when they didn't call it horseback riding. Well, where did they go?
ko: horseback riding이라고 안 부르던 시기가 있었으니까요. 그럼 어디에 올라탔냐면?

en: They used to hold on to the tail...
ko: 꼬리를 잡고...

en: LAUGHTER Are you sure this is how they ride the horses in Europe?
ko: 유럽에서 진짜 그렇게 말 타는 거 맞아요?

en: That's a whole different thing you're doing there, I think.
ko: 그건 완전히 다른 거 하는 거 같은데요.

en: Anybody who's watching this show who may have been flicking the channels, that's not a moment you want to call out.
ko: 이 쇼를 보면서 채널을 돌리던 분이 있다면, 지금 이 순간은 소리 지르면 안 됩니다.

en: No, no, no. This was a story about Americans and descriptive words.
ko: 아니, 아니, 아니요. 이건 미국인들과 설명적인 단어에 대한 이야기였어요.
